Package org.eventb.core.ast.extension

Interface IFormulaExtension

All Known Subinterfaces:
IConstructorExtension, IDestructorExtension, IExpressionExtension, IPredicateExtension, IPredicateExtension2, ITypeConstructorExtension
All Known Implementing Classes:
Cond, ConstructorExtension, DefaultPositionApplicationTests.ExpressionExtension, DefaultPositionApplicationTests.PostfixExpressionExtension, DestructorExtension, ExtendedFormulas.AssociativeExpressionExtension, ExtendedFormulas.CommonExtension, ExtendedFormulas.ExpressionExtension, ExtendedFormulas.PredicateExtension, ExtendedFormulas.PredicateExtension2, ExtendedOperators.AssocExt, ExtensionHelper.AlphaPredicateExtension, ExtensionHelper.BasicFormulaExtension, ExtensionHelper.GenericOperatorExtension, ExtensionHelper.Money, Extensions.AbstractExtension, Extensions.AbstractRealExtension, Extensions.And, Extensions.Belongs, Extensions.CProd, Extensions.Empty, Extensions.FSet, Extensions.Real, Extensions.RealEmpty, Extensions.RealPlus, Extensions.RealZero, Extensions.Union2, Extensions.Union3, ExtensionTranslationTests.Empty, ExtensionTranslationTests.TypeExtension, ExtensionTranslationTests.Weird, Prime, PrimePredicate, TestFactoryCreation.StrangeTypeConstructor, TestFormulaFactory.UnknownExtension, TestGenParser.DummyExtn, TestTypeChecker.Strange, TypeConstructorExtension
public interface IFormulaExtension
Common protocol for formula extensions.

Standard supported extension kinds are provided as constants. Additionally, instances obtained from ExtensionFactory.makePrefixKind(FormulaType, ITypeDistribution) are supported as well, which makes it possible to customize the arity of a parenthesized formula.

For instance, an implementation of getKind() for an expression with three children of type expression, using methods from ExtensionFactory, could be: 

 public IExtensionKind getKind() {
        return makePrefixKind(EXPRESSION, makeAllExpr(makeFixedArity(3)));
 }
which produces an expression extension of kind 'op(a,b,c)'.

Since:
2.0

  • Field Details

    • ATOMIC_EXPRESSION

      static final IExtensionKind ATOMIC_EXPRESSION
      Kind for atomic expressions. An atomic expression is an extended expression that takes no parameter, such as "pred" in the core language.

    • BINARY_INFIX_EXPRESSION

      static final IExtensionKind BINARY_INFIX_EXPRESSION
      Kind for binary infix expressions. A binary infix expression is an extended expression that takes two expressions as parameter, such as "-"" in the core language.

    • ASSOCIATIVE_INFIX_EXPRESSION

      static final IExtensionKind ASSOCIATIVE_INFIX_EXPRESSION
      Kind for associative infix expressions. An associative infix expression is an extended expression that takes at least two expressions as parameters, such as "+" in the core language.

    • PARENTHESIZED_UNARY_EXPRESSION

      static final IExtensionKind PARENTHESIZED_UNARY_EXPRESSION
      Kind for unary prefix expressions. A unary prefix expression is an extended expression that takes one expression as parameter, such as "max"" in the core language. In the concrete syntax, the parameter must be bracketed with parentheses.

    • PARENTHESIZED_BINARY_EXPRESSION

      static final IExtensionKind PARENTHESIZED_BINARY_EXPRESSION
      Kind for binary prefix expressions. A binary prefix expression is an extended expression that takes two expressions as parameters. In the concrete syntax, the parameters must be bracketed with parentheses and separated with a comma.

    • PARENTHESIZED_UNARY_PREDICATE

      static final IExtensionKind PARENTHESIZED_UNARY_PREDICATE
      Kind for unary prefix predicates. A unary prefix predicate is an extended predicate that takes one expression as parameter, such as "finite"" in the core language. In the concrete syntax, the parameter must be bracketed with parentheses.

    • PARENTHESIZED_BINARY_PREDICATE

      static final IExtensionKind PARENTHESIZED_BINARY_PREDICATE
      Kind for binary prefix predicates. A binary prefix predicate is an extended predicate that takes two expressions as parameters. In the concrete syntax, the parameters must be bracketed with parentheses and separated with a comma.

  • Method Details

    • getSyntaxSymbol

      String getSyntaxSymbol()

    • getWDPredicate

      Predicate getWDPredicate(IExtendedFormula formula, IWDMediator wdMediator)

    • conjoinChildrenWD

      boolean conjoinChildrenWD()
      Whether or not children WD is conjoined to the returned WD predicate.

      If true, the resulting WD is 'getWDPredicate() and WD(children)'. If false, the resulting WD is just 'getWDPredicate()'.

      In most cases, children WD shall be conjoined. Reasons not to do so include the case where a WD of the form 'P and ( P => WD(children) )' is desired. In the latter case, it is the responsibility of getWDPredicate(IExtendedFormula, IWDMediator) to explicitly embed the WD conditions for children in the returned predicate.

      Returns:
      true iff children WD is conjoined.

    • getId

      String getId()

      String getGroupId()

    • getKind

    • getOrigin

      Object getOrigin()
      Returns the origin of this extension, or null if no origin is defined.
      Returns:
